mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressures that force monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward quarterly returns remain positive when both GDP growth and CPI exceed their respective 10-year moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain throughput, which increases corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where industrial production increases by more than 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates collapsing consumer demand, which immediately reduces expected corporate earnings and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in >65% of months following a retail sales drop exceeding 2 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting wage growth and consumer spending that produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Monthly forward returns are negative when payroll growth exceeds 200,000 and unemployment falls simultaneously in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains elevated during periods of high inflation, real borrowing costs increase and equity valuations contract, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are positive in >60% of periods where the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0 percent and inflation remains above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal strong builder confidence and rising household formation, which correlates with broader economic optimism and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where housing starts increase by more than 5 percent month-over-month.
